questions.question: What is the total monthly cost of the recommended VA collaboration stack (Slack, Notion, Loom, Google Drive)?
questions.explanation: The entire VA collaboration stack runs under $30/month on free tiers. The free tiers are sufficient until the VA relationship is well-established and the ROI of upgrading is obvious.

questions.question: Why should all task-related VA communication go through a dedicated Slack channel rather than DMs?

options.text: So you have a searchable record and can review conversation history during weekly QA
Because Slack charges extra for DMs
To make the VA feel like part of a team

questions.explanation: Everything goes through #va-channel so you have a searchable record and can review conversation history in your weekly QA. DMs are invisible to QA review and create documentation gaps.

questions.question: Good collaboration tools enable three things. Which is NOT one of them?

options.text: VA can work independently without interrupting you
You can monitor quality without reviewing every output
You can update training when processes change
The VA can make strategic decisions autonomously

questions.explanation: Good tools enable: (1) VA works independently without interrupting you, (2) you monitor quality without reviewing everything, (3) you update training without scheduling calls. Strategic decisions remain the founder's responsibility.

questions.question: You should upgrade to paid collaboration tool tiers before your VA relationship is established.
questions.explanation: False. Start free, upgrade when you have a specific reason. The free tiers are sufficient until the relationship is well-established and the ROI of upgrading is obvious.

questions.question: Design your VA collaboration stack. Which 4-5 tools will you use, what purpose does each serve, and how will you run weekly QA to maintain quality?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response selects specific tools for communication, documentation, video training, and file sharing, defines each tool's purpose, and describes a concrete weekly QA process (scorecard, channel review, output sampling).
